What Is Missing or Not Working: Government support is not enough and is not consistent. Health services are limited, and many people still struggle to get proper treatment. There are not enough toilets in the community, and the existing ones are not well maintained or clean. Also, services are often far away, and many people cannot easily access them when needed.
Why Is This Urgent: because poor healthcare and lack of proper toilets are affecting people’s health every day. Many people get sick easily, and there is a risk of diseases spreading in the community. It is also difficult for women and children especially, and it affects daily life and dignity.
Barriers to Using New Services: Cost of services, long distance to health facilities or toilets, lack of proper information, and sometimes trust in new programs may prevent people from using new services. Some people may also avoid them if they are not well explained or are too far away.
Additional Comments: Our community really needs urgent improvement in healthcare services and sanitation facilities. Building more toilets and improving access to medical care would greatly improve living conditions. Awareness programs and regular support from government or NGOs would also help a lot.

Problem That Affects You Most: Drug or substance abuse and gender based violence
What Is Missing or Not Working: Current government support is not strong enough to fully address drug abuse and gender-based violence. Awareness programs are limited, and many young people are not reached. There is also poor enforcement of laws, and victims often do not get proper protection or counseling. Community support systems are weak, and many cases are not reported because of fear or stigma.
Support That Would Help: Skills training,Women and girls support,Information or awareness programs
Top Priority Problem: Drug or substance abuse and gender based violence
Why Is This Urgent: because drug and substance abuse is increasing among young people, leading to violence, school dropouts, and unsafe communities. Gender-based violence is also affecting the safety and wellbeing of women and girls. If not addressed quickly, it will continue to harm families and the future of young people in the community.
Additional Comments: We needs more awareness programs, especially for young people, to educate them about the dangers of drug abuse and gender-based violence. There should also be stronger support systems for victims, including counseling services and safe spaces. More involvement from government, NGOs, and community leaders would help reduce these problems. Creating job opportunities and skills training for youth could also reduce drug use and improve behavior in the community.

What Is Missing or Not Working: Government support is not strong enough and does not fully protect traders and small businesses. There is poor security in the community and at the market, so theft is common. Financial support like loans or small business assistance is not available for many people. Prices are also unstable, and there is no proper system to help small traders grow their businesses safely.
Why Is This Urgent: I am a 67-year-old woman doing a small business to support myself, but I am not getting enough support or assistance to grow my business. People are also stealing from me in the market, which makes it very difficult for me to survive. This situation is urgent because I depend on my small business for my daily living, and without proper support and security, I continue to suffer losses that affect my basic needs.
Barriers to Using New Services: High cost of services, lack of trust in programs, and poor access to information
Additional Comments: The community needs stronger security, especially in markets, to stop theft. Small traders like myself need financial support or small loans to improve our businesses. If government or NGOs can help with business training and better protection, it would greatly improve our lives.

Problem That Affects You Most: Unemployment and limited access to digital skills and opportunities affect me the most personally. It makes it difficult to secure stable income and grow professionally, which is why I am actively seeking learning and career development opportunities.
Current Solutions: Government institutions offer policies, limited funding opportunities, public programs, and education initiatives, although access and reach can still be limited.

Respondent Age: Many programs do not reach people in remote or underserved communities. Support is often concentrated in cities, leaving rural populations without help.
Community / Area: The top priority problem that should be solved first is youth unemployment and lack of digital skills opportunities.
Occupation: This problem is urgent because a large number of young people in our community are finishing school but are unable to find jobs or start sustainable businesses. Many do not have access to digital skills training, internet resources, mentorship, or funding opportunities. As a result, talented youth remain idle, underemployed, or forced into low-income informal work.
Problem That Affects You Most: Cost and affordability – Many people may assume the program is expensive or may not afford transport, internet, or participation fees.
Current Solutions: Our community has many talented and motivated young people who are eager to learn, work, and build better futures, but they lack access to opportunities, mentorship, and resources. What would help most is long-term support rather than one-time interventions. Sustainable programs that provide continuous training, mentorship, internet access, and small startup support can create lasting impact.
